content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, we'll explore how to use control structures in Jinja2 templates within Flask applications. 

Control structures allow you to add logic and dynamic behavior to your HTML templates, making them more powerful and flexible.
1. Loops: \
Jinja2 provides a 'for' loop to iterate over sequences like lists or dictionaries. \
This is useful for rendering dynamic content, such as displaying items from a database. \
Syntax: 
```html
{% for user in users %}
    {{ user.name }}
{% endfor %}
```
2. Conditionals: \
You can use 'if' statements to add conditional logic to your templates. \
This allows you to display different content based on certain conditions. \
Syntax:
```html
{% if user.is_admin %}
    Welcome, Admin!
{% else %}
    Welcome, User!
{% endif %}
```
3. Filters: \
Filters in Jinja2 allow you to modify variables before they are displayed. \
They are applied using the pipe symbol (|).

Syntax:
```html
{{ username | upper }}  
{{ price | round(2) }}  
```

Remember, when using these control structures, make sure to pass the necessary data from your Flask route to the template using the render_template() function, as we learned in earlier lessons.
